pass1.intended_concept: Mitochondrial chemiosmosis: ETC establishes a proton gradient; ATP synthase couples H+ return to ATP formation
pass1.item_skill: Read membrane diagram and justify where H+ concentration is higher relative to the matrix
visual_features_required: inner mitochondrial membrane with embedded complexes
ATP synthase depicted as a channel through the inner membrane
intermembrane space and matrix regions distinguishable
directional proton pumping consistent with textbook ETC layout
forbidden_mismatches: figure with no ATP synthase or no inner membrane
figure labeled only as photosystem Z-scheme
figure showing only ATP synthase with no ETC proton-pumping complexes visible
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: The keyed answer requires students to connect higher H+ in the intermembrane space to ETC pumping shown in the diagram, not generic recall alone.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure 7.4 (variant): ETC, proton pumping into the intermembrane space, and chemiosmosis through ATP synthase (OpenStax Biology 2e bundle media filename Figure_07_04_03-5144.png).

actual_visual_read.figure_type: mitochondrial inner membrane: electron transport chain plus proton gradient and ATP synthase
visible_features: inner membrane with ETC complexes (I–IV) and mobile carriers
H⁺ pumping from matrix into intermembrane space
higher H⁺ concentration implied or shown in intermembrane space
ATP synthase with H⁺ return flow toward matrix
electron path from NADH/FADH₂ toward O₂

pass1.intended_concept: Chloroplast compartments: thylakoids/grana vs stroma for Calvin cycle location
pass1.item_skill: Locate Calvin-cycle enzymes using chloroplast structure
visual_features_required: chloroplast cutaway or diagram
visible thylakoid stacks / grana
stroma region distinguishable from thylakoid lumen
structure suitable for asking stroma vs thylakoid-only
forbidden_mismatches: Calvin cycle pathway chart with no chloroplast outline
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: Students must use spatial structure (stroma surrounding grana) to choose where Calvin enzymes operate.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure 8.1 (variant): Chloroplast overview — light reactions vs Calvin cycle in stroma (bundle media Figure_08_01_06-40d9.jpg; do not substitute other Figure_08_02_01* filenames).

actual_visual_read.figure_type: chloroplast diagram with granum stacks, stroma, and Calvin cycle region
visible_features: light-dependent vs Calvin regions as one overview

pass1.intended_concept: Calvin cycle: reduction phase consumes ATP and NADPH
pass1.item_skill: Identify Calvin-cycle stage that uses ATP and NADPH from the diagram
visual_features_required: three-stage Calvin depiction (fixation, reduction, regeneration) or equivalent labeled stages
reduction stage visually tied to ATP/NADPH inputs
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: The question names Calvin stages; the figure must show those stages so the reduction stage is identifiable.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure 8.3: Calvin cycle with labeled phases (bundle media Figure_08_03_02.png; Figure_08_03_05.png is unrelated genetics in this bundle).

actual_visual_read.figure_type: Calvin cycle overview with three labeled phases
visible_features: ATP and NADPH inputs to reduction

Q06.source_section_title: 8.3 Using light energy to make organic molecules

pass1.intended_concept: Citric acid (Krebs) cycle location in mitochondria
pass1.item_skill: Identify compartment for Krebs from pathway diagram
visual_features_required: citric acid cycle pathway or mitochondrial matrix context
labels or layout indicating matrix vs intermembrane space / inner membrane
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: The answer (matrix) must be justified from the figure’s mitochondrial context, not from recall alone.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure 7.3 (variant): Citric acid cycle (bundle media Figure_07_03_02.jpg).

actual_visual_read.figure_type: citric acid cycle pathway in mitochondrial matrix
visible_features: mitochondrial matrix labeling or implied compartment

Q08.source_section_title: 7.3 Oxidation of pyruvate and the citric acid cycle

pass1.intended_concept: Aerobic respiration overview: largest net ATP from oxidative phosphorylation
pass1.item_skill: Read overview map and identify stage with most net ATP per glucose
visual_features_required: four labeled stages in aerobic sequence ending in oxidative phosphorylation (glycolysis through ETC)
visual emphasis or logical placement showing oxidative phosphorylation as the culmination of aerobic glucose catabolism
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: The stem asks which stage dominates net ATP; the overview diagram supplies the comparative structure.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure (Ch. 7.6 metabolic connections): aerobic respiration flow — glycolysis, pyruvate oxidation, citric acid cycle, oxidative phosphorylation (bundle media Figure_07_06_02.png).

actual_visual_read.figure_type: horizontal aerobic respiration stage map plus nutrient-entry callouts
visible_features: Glycolysis → Pyruvate oxidation → Citric acid cycle → Oxidative phosphorylation (boxed sequence)
optional nutrient-entry labels feeding early stages (carbohydrate/amino acids/glycerol, fatty acids, etc.)

Q12.source_section_title: 7.6 Connections of carbohydrate, protein, and lipid metabolic pathways

pass1.intended_concept: Action spectrum / Engelmann-style reasoning: photosynthetic rate vs wavelength
visual_features_required: multiple panels comparing illumination conditions or wavelengths
filament / bacterial-indicator layout consistent with Engelmann-style reasoning
forbidden_mismatches: single absorption spectrum graph without panels/filament context
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: The stem references panels and bacterial clustering; the figure must show that comparative layout.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure 8.2 (variant): Action spectrum / Engelmann-style panels (bundle media Figure_08_02_05abcd-d386.jpg).

visible_features: filament with bacterial clusters differing by region

pass1.intended_concept: Light reactions output ATP and NADPH for Calvin cycle
pass1.item_skill: Identify outputs of thylakoid electron flow from diagram
visual_features_required: photosystems and/or thylakoid ETC depiction
ATP and NADPH shown as products feeding Calvin cycle (or clearly implied)
forbidden_mismatches: Calvin cycle only with no photosystems
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: The keyed answer (ATP and NADPH) must be visible products in the light-reaction diagram.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure 8.2 (variant): Light reactions — photosystems and thylakoid electron transport (bundle media Figure_08_02_08-e4a1.jpg; Figure_08_02_06.png is unrelated genetics in this bundle).

actual_visual_read.figure_type: photosystems with electron transport in thylakoid membrane

pass1.intended_concept: Photosynthesis summary: CO2 and H2O inputs; glucose and O2 outputs; link to gas cycling
pass1.item_skill: Connect summary diagram to global CO2/O2 cycling language
visual_features_required: summary-level photosynthesis equation or reactant/product diagram
CO2 and H2O as inputs and O2 (and carbohydrate) as outputs
forbidden_mismatches: leaf cross-section with no reaction summary
pass1.why_the_image_is_needed_for_this_item: The stem references what the summary diagram emphasizes about inputs/outputs; students must read that summary.

pass2.figure_caption_verified: Figure 8.1 — summary of photosynthesis (bundle media Figure_08_01_04.jpg).
